titleStmt.title: Letter from Major E. Reade to Mabel FitzGerald 4 August, 1916.
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: E. Reade

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: Desmond FitzGerald (1888-1947), was an Irish Volunteer and was present in the GPO during the Easter Rising. He was subsequently arrested, court-martialed and sentenced to 20 years Penal Servitude, later commuted to 10 years. Mabel FitzGerald (1884-1957), was the wife of Desmond FitzGerald. This letter was written while Desmond FitzGerald was a prisoner in Dartmoor. Reade, acknowledges Mrs. FitzGerald's letter of 2 August and indicates a reply will be sent in a few days.

correspAction.placeName: 2 Loretto Villas, Bray, Co. Wicklow, Ireland.

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
All Communications should be
addressed to "The Governor,"
and not to any official by name. P80/1607C2) H. M. Prison,
Dartmoor 4 Aug 1916. Madam I have to acknowledge the receipt of your letter of the 2nd Aug
and to inform you in reply that a reply will be sent
in a few days I am,
Madam
Your obedient Servant,
E. Reade
Governor. Mrs Fitzgerald
2 Loretta Villas
Bray
Ireland
